entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Groundnut or peanut (Arachis hypogaea), a crop affected by late leaf spot

entities.name: LATE LEAF SPOT
entities.description: A foliar disease of groundnut caused by Phaeoisariopsis personata

entities.name: PHAEOISARIOPSIS PERSONATA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes late leaf spot in groundnut

entities.name: BACILLUS CIRCULANS GRS 243
entities.description: An antifungal and chitinolytic bacterium used for the biological control of late leaf spot

entities.name: SERRATIA MARCESCENS GPS 5
entities.description: An antifungal and chitinolytic bacterium used for the biological control of late leaf spot

entities.name: PROPHYLACTIC FOLIAR SPRAY
entities.description: A preventative application method used to apply bacteria to the foliage of the crop

entities.name: CHITIN-SUPPLEMENTED APPLICATION
entities.description: The application of bacterial cells supplemented with colloidal chitin to enhance disease control

entities.name: COLLOIDAL CHITIN
entities.description: A supplement used to improve the efficacy of biological control agents

entities.name: LESION FREQUENCY
entities.description: A measure of disease severity used to evaluate the effectiveness of LLS control

entities.name: POD YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of the groundnut crop measured by pod yield

entities.name: KHARIF
entities.description: The rainy season during which on-farm trials were conducted

entities.name: CHITINASE
entities.description: A 55-kDa enzyme purified from S. marcescens GPS 5 that inhibits the germination of P. personata conidia

entities.name: AFFINITY CHROMATOGRAPHY
entities.description: A purification technique used to isolate chitinase from culture filtrate

entities.name: GEL FILTRATION
entities.description: A purification technique used to isolate chitinase from culture filtrate

entities.name: CHITINOLYSIS
entities.description: The process of breaking down chitin, which is critical for the biological control of LLS

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL CONTROL
entities.description: The use of biological agents to suppress the population of a pathogen
